question: Guruji why do we get old emotions like anger resurfacing? How do we get over them?
answer:
Namaskaram 

What an excellent question to begin this sacred Ugadi…
And on this new year, let me share something little deep and profound with you a truth that is absolute… 

Old emotions like anger do not resurface without reason. They return because they were never completely burnt in awareness they were only buried under time, distraction, or temporary calm.

On the path of sadhana, especially when one begins to walk closer to Bhairava, many hidden layers of the inner being start coming up. What was lying in the subconscious begins to rise. This is not a punishment, and it is not a sign that you are failing. In many cases, it is actually a sign that the inner cleansing has begun.

Bhairava does not only give peace — He first reveals what is false, what is wounded, what is suppressed, and what still binds you.

So when anger rises, do not immediately think, “Why is this happening to me again?”
Instead ask, “What is Bhairava showing me through this?”

Anger is very rarely the root. Behind anger there is usually hurt, expectation, fear, rejection, control, or an old samskara that has not yet been dissolved. The outer situation is only the trigger. The real work is inside.

Do not suppress the anger.
Do not express it blindly.
Do not justify it through the mind.

Instead, witness it… 

Sit quietly. Watch how it rises in the body. Watch how it burns in the breath, in the chest, in the thoughts. Become the one who sees it, not the one who becomes it. The moment witnessing begins, the identification starts weakening.

This is where sadhana becomes real not when everything is peaceful, but when the inner storm comes and you remain present.

Tell yourself within:
“This is arising to be seen, not to be fed. This is coming up to leave, not to define me.”

Remember this deeply 
What resurfaces in sadhana is often not a fall… it is a purification.
Guru Bhairava is bringing hidden poison to the surface so it can be released consciously.

In Bhairava’s path, even resurfacing darkness is grace because only what comes to the surface can finally be dissolved. 

~ Jai Bhairava, Jai Maa Kamakhya

question:
I have read document on sadhana but have few questions.
1. If I decide for first Mandala for 48 days, how many times should I chant Bhairava Naam japa?
2. Do I need to follow all steps from sadhana document on daily basis (ex. Achamana, Sankalp & Bhog)?
3. Do I need to complete sadhana on same time? Because in case I am traveling in the evening, then can I do sadhana in morning?
4. Will my above Japa count considered in total japa count ex all other sankalpa/nitya japa
answer:
Namaskaram 

Thank you for reading the sadhana document so sincerely and for asking these questions with honesty. This itself shows your intent to walk properly, not casually.

Please understand one very important thing very clearly — this is not an optional practice-based checklist, this is a Sankalpa sadhana.
That means each step shared in the document carries purpose and energy. It is not just “extra ritual detail.”
If we start selecting only what feels convenient and leave the rest, then slowly the path becomes more like spiritual tourism rather than true upasana.

So yes, every step should be taken into consideration with sincerity achamana, sankalpa, bhog, japa discipline, closure, all of it matters because together they create the container in which Bhairava tattva begins to respond.

Regarding your questions:
1. For the first mandala (48 days), do the Bhairava Naam japa exactly as shared in the sadhana document / sankalpa given. Do not randomly increase or reduce based on mood. In sankalpa sadhana, consistency is more important than excitement.

2. Yes, all steps should ideally be followed daily.
These are not optional add-ons. They prepare, invoke, anchor, and offer. That is what makes it upasana, not just repetition of a name.

3. Same time is always best, because regularity builds a strong energetic rhythm.
But if genuinely required due to travel or unavoidable life situations, you may do it in the morning instead of evening on that day.
The key is: do not skip, do not become casual, and do not keep changing timings unnecessarily. Exceptions should remain exceptions.

4. Yes, this japa can be counted in your total japa count, but during this mandala, mentally remember that this is not just “another count.”
This is sankalpa-bound japa, so it should be done with that bhava and discipline, not mixed casually like routine nitya japa.

If you walk this as a vow, Bhairava sees it differently.
If you walk this casually, the experience also remains casual.

Sadhana gives according to the seriousness of the one who enters it.
Do it as an upasaka, not as a visitor.

~ Jai Bhairava

question: Guruji why do we think or listen to kaali while praying to bhairava
answer:
Namaskaram 

This is a very beautiful and important question… let me explain little bit more on essence of this! 

In Bhairava sadhana, do not think that Maa and Bhairava are separate. In truth, where Bhairava is invoked with sincerity, Shakti will naturally reveal herself. That is why many Bhairava Sadhakas, during prayer or japa, may feel drawn toward Maa in one form or another.

Sometimes she may come as Kaali, sometimes as Tara, sometimes as Bhairavi, and for some even as Tripurasundari. This is not confusion in sadhana this is the living movement of Shakti responding to the depth of your inner connection.

But understand this carefully:
all these are not different in essence. They are different revelations of the same Adi Shakti.

And let me tell you very clearly all Dasha Mahavidyas are deeply connected to Maa Kamakhya. She is Sampoorna Shakti, the complete womb of manifestation, the totality from which all these Mahavidya swaroopas arise. Kaali, Tara, Bhairavi, Chinnamasta, Dhumavati, Bagalamukhi, Matangi, Kamala, Bhuvaneshwari, Tripurasundari all are rays of that one complete Mahashakti.

So if during Bhairava Upasana, Maa’s presence is felt in any swaroopa, receive it with reverence, not with doubt. It means the sadhana is becoming alive within you.

However and this is very important in the path of Sankalpa sadhana do not keep shifting your practice based on experiences.
Experience is grace, but discipline is the vessel that holds grace.

So remain firm in the mantra, method, and sankalpa given for Bhairava sadhana.
Let Bhairava remain your axis.
If Maa reveals herself, bow with gratitude… but do not become spiritually restless and jump from one swaroopa to another.

A true Upasaka does not run behind every spiritual sensation.
A true Bhairava Sadhaka remains anchored, and then both Bhairava and Shakti unfold themselves in the right time.

So yes … hearing, feeling, or being inwardly drawn to Kaali while praying to Bhairava is natural. And over time, Maa may reveal herself in many forms. But stay rooted in your Bhairava Sankalpa. That steadiness is what makes the path open fully.

~ Jai Bhairava

question: After I finish my cycle and do the head bath do I add 5-7 days to my mandala sadhana or do I keep going from the last day I started my cycle from
answer:
Namaskaram Maa, 

It purely depends on how your energy levels are during that time and how restful or stable you feel within yourself.

For many, during those days, the energy naturally becomes half or inward, so I usually suggest they may pause the sankalpa count and simply continue the japa without counting it in the Mandala Sankalpa. After completion and head bath, they can continue the Mandala from where they paused.

But if you genuinely feel that your energy, connection, and inner steadiness are strong, then you may absolutely continue, and those days can be counted within the complete Mandala sadhana.

So in the end, it is not a rigid rule it is purely about the connection between you, your energy, and your Sankalpa.
Listen to your body, but remain honest to the sadhana. 

~ Jai Bhairava

question: Can Maa Kamakhya sādhana support relationship issues, marriage delays, harmony, and emotional healing — or is it only a sādhana for wealth?
answer:
Bhuvaneshwari G Hunashyal - 

Namaskaram 

Let me share here as this tap is exclusive for Q & A.

Please do not see Maa Kamakhya only as a sadhana for wealth.
That is only one small aspect of her kripa.

Maa is Karunamayi. She is Kamakhya.
She blesses with Kama Sampanna, the fulfillment of sincere desires and rightful needs.
So yes, this sadhana can absolutely support relationship issues, marriage delays, harmony, emotional healing, family bonding, along with abundance.

But please remember one thing deeply 
Do not approach Maa only as a solution… approach her as Shakti.
When approached with bhava, gratitude, and true surrender,
She grants not only what we ask… but what is truly needed.

And let me remind everyone ere very clearly…

Doing sadhana only with a reason, an agenda, or fixed expectation is like parenting a child with conditions and personal motives.
That is not pure love… that is attachment with expectation.

Do not approach Maa with an agenda. Approach her with love.
Do not approach her with demand. Approach her with surrender.

If we choose only from our current desires,
we are choosing from limited understanding, limited vision, and limited resources.
But when we surrender to Maa,
She chooses from divine drishti, perfect timing, and deeper grace.

So just proceed with the sadhana with bhava, gratitude, and samarpana.
She knows what to give, when to give, where to open, and how to move things in your life.

Trust her more than your own planning.
That is where real kripa begins.

Jai Bhairava 
Jai Maa Kamakhya.

question:
Guruji after connecting with bhairav baba.. I am inclined towards ma tripura, sometimes now I feel connection towards ma kali, sometimes bhairavi, kamakhya.. Also to ma varahi.. I am so confused..
Guruji.. Thankyou so much for ma kamakhya sadhana.. I wanted to ask
1) can I start from this coming Tuesday??

Do not be confused this is not confusion, this is Maa calling you through many doors.

Whether it is Tripura, Kali, Bhairavi, Varahi, or Kamakhya
the forms are different, but the Moola Shakti is One.

Irrespective of any form, She is Kamakhya only.
